description: Add a rebase-all subcommand that pulls origin/main into the main worktree then rebases all gbiv-managed color worktrees onto origin/main.

userStories.title: Move find_repo_in_worktree to git_utils
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to move the private find_repo_in_worktree function from status.rs to git_utils.rs so that the rebase-all command can reuse it.
acceptanceCriteria: find_repo_in_worktree is defined as pub fn in src/git_utils.rs
src/commands/status.rs imports find_repo_in_worktree from crate::git_utils instead of defining it locally
cargo build passes with no errors or warnings

userStories.title: Create rebase_all.rs with scaffold and pull-main logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create src/commands/rebase_all.rs with rebase_all_command() that locates the gbiv root and main worktree, runs git pull, and aborts with an error if the pull fails.
acceptanceCriteria: src/commands/rebase_all.rs exists with a pub fn rebase_all_command() -> Result<(), String>
The function calls find_gbiv_root to locate the repo root
The function locates the main worktree using find_repo_in_worktree on the main/ directory
The function runs git pull in the main worktree directory
If git pull fails, the function returns Err with a descriptive message and does not proceed
cargo build passes

userStories.title: Implement color worktree rebase loop and result output
userStories.description: As a developer, I need rebase_all_command() to iterate over all COLORS in order, rebase each existing worktree onto origin/main, and print a color-coded per-worktree result line.
acceptanceCriteria: Iterates COLORS in order: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet
Skips any color whose worktree directory does not exist on disk
If a worktree is already mid-rebase (i.e. .git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply exists inside the repo), skips it with a SKIP (rebase in progress) status line and does not attempt a new rebase
Runs git rebase origin/main in each worktree that is not already mid-rebase
On rebase conflict, leaves the worktree in the conflicted state (does NOT run git rebase --abort) and continues to the next color
Prints a result line per worktree using existing ANSI color helpers, indicating success, failure, or skip
Returns Err if one or more worktrees failed to rebase
cargo build passes
userStories.notes: Behavior change: removed git rebase --abort on conflict (leave worktree in conflicted state instead). Added: skip worktrees already mid-rebase.

userStories.title: Wire rebase-all subcommand into the CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to register the rebase-all subcommand in the CLI so that users can invoke gbiv rebase-all.
acceptanceCriteria: pub mod rebase_all; is added to src/commands/mod.rs
A rebase-all subcommand with about text is registered in the cli() function in src/main.rs
A Some(("rebase-all", _)) dispatch arm in main() calls rebase_all_command() and handles the Err case with eprintln + process::exit(1)
cargo build passes

userStories.title: Verify rebase-all builds and runs correctly
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to confirm that gbiv rebase-all compiles cleanly and behaves correctly in a real gbiv repo.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo build completes with zero errors and zero warnings
gbiv rebase-all --help displays the subcommand description
Running gbiv rebase-all in a gbiv repo prints a result line for each color worktree
